name: La Muela from La Garganta through El Puertecito
name_original: La Muela desde La Garganta por El Puertecito
region_intro: Hiking around La Garganta offers access to a diverse mountain ecosystem within the Sierra de Candelario, characterized by natural monuments, mountain peaks, and deep canyons. The region features deciduous forests and riverine woodlands, with elevations ranging significantly. Water has carved dramatic scenery, creating varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around La Garganta The most popular hiking route is Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical paths with natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Muela – circular by the Turbal de Navamuño - Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario, a difficult 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail offers views of the Sierra de Candelario. Local hikers also love the Peña Negra and Embalse de Navamuño Loop, a 8.2 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through mountain terrain and past the Embalse de Navamuño, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around La Garganta is defined by mountain peaks, canyons, and deciduous forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in La Garganta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Garganta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Garganta?
A: La Garganta offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with nearly 150 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes in La Garganta?
A: The terrain around La Garganta is incredibly diverse, shaped by its location within the Sierra de Candelario. You'll find trails winding through deciduous forests, along riverine woodlands, past natural monuments, and up to mountain peaks. The dramatic scenery is often carved by water, creating varied landscapes including deep canyons and open mountain areas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners in La Garganta?
A: Yes, La Garganta has 28 easy hiking routes. A great option for a gentle introduction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990854911/banos-de-montemayor-via-verde-and-calzada-romana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.9 km) long and combines historical paths with natural scenery.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the La Garganta area?
A: For experienced hikers, La Garganta offers 30 difficult routes. One challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990347612/hoya-moros-and-canchal-de-la-ceja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya Moros and Canchal de la Ceja Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 10.3-mile (16.6 km) trail with significant elevation gain, leading through rugged mountain terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in La Garganta?
A: Hikers in La Garganta can explore a wealth of natural features. You might encounter stunning gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hell's Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or visit beautiful water bodies such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2081643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya Moros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Garganta?
A: Yes, many routes in La Garganta are designed as loops. An example of a circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993360254/la-muela-circular-by-the-turbal-de-navamuno-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-candelario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muela – circular by the Turbal de Navamuño - Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 6.2-mile (10.0 km) path offering views of the Sierra de Candelario.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Garganta?
A: The trails in La Garganta are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 out of 5 stars from over 360 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse mountain ecosystem, the dramatic scenery carved by water, and the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: Is La Garganta a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: While the guide data doesn't specifically tag routes as 'family-friendly,' the presence of 28 easy routes suggests there are suitable options for families. These shorter, less strenuous trails, often through forests or along historical paths, would be ideal for outings with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in La Garganta?
A: The region of La Garganta, part of Extremadura, is generally welcoming to dogs on trails, especially in natural areas like the Sierra de Candelario. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check for specific local regulations or protected zones that might have restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Garganta?
A: Given the region's deciduous forests and riverine woodlands, spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking in La Garganta. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming flora, while autumn offers vibrant fall colors. Summers can be hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winters can be cold with potential for snow at higher elevations (up to 2342 meters).

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views in La Garganta?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of La Garganta provides several opportunities for panoramic views. The region research mentions trails offering views of the Sierra de Béjar and the picturesque Ambroz Valley. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight that offers expansive vistas.

Q: How long do hikes in La Garganta typically take?
A: Hike durations vary significantly based on the route's difficulty and length. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990854911/banos-de-montemayor-via-verde-and-calzada-romana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. More challenging trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993356698/pena-negra-and-embalse-de-navamuno-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra and Embalse de Navamuño Loop&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 4 hours.

Perfect peak from which to see it from the Sierra de Candelario to the Sierra de Francia, passing through Extremadura. Around the peak there is a ferrata route that can be done with the help of a harness.
